name: retail_sales_enriched
description: Retail sales regression problem built from a 5-table star schema. The target is the total transaction amount. Five source files must be joined before running the pipeline: sales_transactions.csv (fact), dim_customers.csv (join: customer_id), dim_products.csv (join: product_id), dim_stores.csv (join: store_id), dim_brands.csv (join: brand_id via dim_products). An additional non-obvious join exists between dim_customers.city_code and dim_stores.city_code (shared geographic attribute). The merged dataset has 500 rows and 24 feature columns. This scenario is used to validate join discovery across diverse key types: integer FKs, an indirect FK (brand_id not in fact table), and a shared string attribute (city_code).

columns.name: transaction_id
columns.description: Unique transaction identifier. Source: sales_transactions.csv.

columns.name: sale_date
columns.description: Date of the transaction (YYYY-MM-DD). Not a join key. Source: sales_transactions.csv.

columns.name: quantity
columns.description: Number of units purchased. Source: sales_transactions.csv.

columns.name: unit_price
columns.description: Price per unit at time of sale in euros. Source: sales_transactions.csv.

columns.name: discount_rate
columns.description: Fractional discount applied (0.0–0.20). Source: sales_transactions.csv.

columns.name: total_amount
columns.description: Final transaction value in euros: quantity × unit_price × (1 − discount_rate). TARGET COLUMN. Source: sales_transactions.csv.

columns.name: channel
columns.description: Sales channel. Source: sales_transactions.csv.

columns.name: cust_age_group
columns.description: Customer age bracket. Source: dim_customers.csv (joined on customer_id).

columns.name: cust_city_code
columns.description: 3-letter city code of the customer's home city. Source: dim_customers.csv (joined on customer_id). Also joinable to store_city_code via shared geography.

columns.name: cust_loyalty_tier
columns.description: Customer loyalty programme tier. Source: dim_customers.csv (joined on customer_id).

columns.name: cust_since_year
columns.description: Year the customer first registered. Source: dim_customers.csv (joined on customer_id).

columns.name: prod_category
columns.description: Top-level product category. Source: dim_products.csv (joined on product_id).

columns.name: prod_subcategory
columns.description: Product tier within the category. Source: dim_products.csv (joined on product_id).

columns.name: prod_base_price
columns.description: Recommended retail price in euros. Source: dim_products.csv (joined on product_id).

columns.name: prod_weight_kg
columns.description: Product weight in kilograms. Source: dim_products.csv (joined on product_id).

columns.name: store_city_code
columns.description: 3-letter city code of the store. Source: dim_stores.csv (joined on store_id). Also joinable to cust_city_code via shared geography.

columns.name: store_region
columns.description: Spanish geographic region of the store. Source: dim_stores.csv (joined on store_id).

columns.name: store_format
columns.description: Store format by size. Source: dim_stores.csv (joined on store_id).

columns.name: store_sq_meters
columns.description: Sales floor area in square metres. Source: dim_stores.csv (joined on store_id).

columns.name: brand_name
columns.description: Commercial brand name. Source: dim_brands.csv (joined via dim_products.brand_id — indirect join not in fact table).

columns.name: brand_country_of_origin
columns.description: ISO 2-letter country code of the brand's founding country. Source: dim_brands.csv (indirect join).

columns.name: brand_market_segment
columns.description: Price positioning of the brand. Source: dim_brands.csv (indirect join).

columns.name: brand_founded_year
columns.description: Year the brand was established. Source: dim_brands.csv (indirect join).
